You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 410d8849).

## Issue to Resolve

Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Autoformatting should not reformat below line to incorrect state
```
private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Observed Behavior
Autoformatting moves `DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))` to the next line and as the results it looks like this:
```
    private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = 
    DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Autoformatting should allow to reproduce this. My Detekt config:
```
formatting:
    excludes: [ '**/build/**' ]
    MaximumLineLength:
        active: false
    NoBlankLineBeforeRbrace:
        active: false
    Indentation:
        active: false
    ArgumentListWrapping:
        active: false
    MultiLineIfElse:
        active: false
```

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.1
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used: Detekt 1.23.0. Related issue https://github.com/detekt/detekt/issues/6223
* Version of Gradle: 7.3.3
* Kotlin version 1.8.21

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 26→import mu.KotlinLogging
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→
 29→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}
172→
173→public val PROPERTY_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyWrappingRule().ruleId
174→

263→/**
264→ * Updates or inserts a new whitespace element with [text] after the given node. If the node itself is a whitespace
265→ * then its contents is replaced with [text]. If the node is a (nested) composite element, the whitespace element is
266→ * added after the last child leaf.

268→public fun ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(text: String) {
269→    if (this is LeafElement) {
270→        if (this.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
271→            return replaceWhitespaceWith(text)
272→        }
273→        val next = treeNext ?: nextLeaf()
274→        if (next != null && next.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
275→            next.replaceWhitespaceWith(text)
276→        } else {
277→            PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(text).also { psiWhiteSpace ->
278→                (psi as LeafElement).rawInsertAfterMe(psiWhiteSpace)
279→            }
280→        }
281→    } else {
282→        lastChildLeafOrSelf().upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(text)
283→    }

286→public val ASTNode.column: Int
287→    get() {
288→        var leaf = this.prevLeaf()
289→        var offsetToTheLeft = 0
290→        while (leaf != null) {
291→            if ((leaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE || leaf.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART) && leaf.textContains('\n')) {
292→                offsetToTheLeft += leaf.textLength - 1 - leaf.text.lastIndexOf('\n')
293→                break
294→            }
295→            offsetToTheLeft += leaf.textLength
296→            leaf = leaf.prevLeaf()
297→        }

329→/**
330→ * Verifies that a leaf containing a newline exist in the closed range [from] - [to]. Also, returns true in case any of
331→ * the boundary nodes [from] or [to] contains a newline.
332→ */
333→public fun hasNewLineInClosedRange(
334→    from: ASTNode,
335→    to: ASTNode,
336→): Boolean =
337→    from.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() ||
338→        leavesInOpenRange(from, to).any { it.textContains('\n') } ||
339→        to.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→
341→/**
342→ * Verifies that no leaf contains a newline in the closed range [from] - [to]. Also, the boundary nodes [from] and [to]
343→ * should not contain a newline.
344→ */
345→public fun noNewLineInClosedRange(
346→    from: ASTNode,
347→    to: ASTNode,
348→): Boolean =
349→    !from.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
350→        noNewLineInOpenRange(from, to) &&
351→        !to.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
352→
353→/**
354→ * Verifies that no leaf contains a newline in the open range [from] - [to]. This means that the boundary nodes are excluded from the range
355→ * in case they would happen to be a leaf node. In case [from] is a [CompositeElement] than the first leaf node in the sequence is the first
356→ * leaf node in this [CompositeElement]. In case [to] is a [CompositeElement] than the last node in the sequence is the last leaf node prior
357→ * to this [CompositeElement].
358→ */
359→public fun noNewLineInOpenRange(
360→    from: ASTNode,
361→    to: ASTNode,
362→): Boolean = leavesInOpenRange(from, to).none { it.textContains('\n') }
363→
364→/**
365→ * Creates a sequence of leaf nodes in the open range [from] - [to]. This means that the boundary nodes are excluded
366→ * from the range in case they would happen to be a leaf node. In case [from] is a [CompositeElement] than the first
367→ * leaf node in the sequence is the first leaf node in this [CompositeElement]. In case [to] is a [CompositeElement]
368→ * than the last node in the sequence is the last leaf node prior to this [CompositeElement].
369→ */
370→public fun leavesInOpenRange(
371→    from: ASTNode,
372→    to: ASTNode,
373→): Sequence<ASTNode> =

 90→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 91→                ?: node
 92→
 93→        node
 94→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 95→            ?.let { colon ->
 96→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 97→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 98→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 99→                    return
100→                }
101→            }
102→
103→        node
104→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
105→            ?.let { typeReference ->
106→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
107→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
108→                    return
109→                }
110→            }
111→
112→        node
113→            .findChildByType(EQ)
114→            ?.let { equal ->
115→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
116→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
117→                    return
118→                }
119→            }
120→
121→        node
122→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
123→            ?.let { callExpression ->
124→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
125→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
126→                    return
127→                }
128→            }
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
132→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
133→            .nextCodeLeaf()
134→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
135→            ?: this
136→
137→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
138→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
139→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
140→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
141→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
142→    }
143→
144→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
145→        node: ASTNode,
146→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
147→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
148→    ) {
149→        emit(
150→            node.startOffset - 1,
151→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
152→            true,
153→        )
154→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
155→        if (autoCorrect) {
156→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
157→        }
158→    }
159→
160→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
161→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
162→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
163→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
164→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
165→    ) {
166→        emit(
167→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
168→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
169→            true,
170→        )
171→        LOGGER.trace {
172→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
173→        }
174→        if (autoCorrect) {
175→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
176→        }
177→    }
178→}
179→
180→public val PARAMETER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterWrappingRule().ruleId

0→# Changelog
 1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 3→
 4→## Unreleased
 5→
 6→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 7→
 8→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 9→
10→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
11→
12→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
13→
14→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
15→
16→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
17→
18→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
19→
20→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
21→
22→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
23→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
24→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
25→
26→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
27→
28→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
29→
30→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
31→
32→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
33→
34→### Added
35→
36→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
37→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
38→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
39→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
40→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
41→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
42→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
43→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
44→
45→### Removed
46→
47→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
48→
49→### Fixed
50→
51→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
52→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
53→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
54→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
55→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
56→
57→### Changed
58→
59→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
60→* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
61→* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
62→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
63→* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
64→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
65→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
66→
67→## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
68→
69→### Added
70→
71→### Removed
72→
73→### Fixed
74→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
75→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
76→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
77→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
78→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
79→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))

Summary:
- Updated `PropertyWrappingRule` and `ParameterWrappingRule` so that newlines inserted before wrapped nodes now use `IndentConfig.childIndentOf(...)`, preserving the expected child indentation even when the `IndentationRule` is disabled.
- Added `PropertyWrappingRuleBestEffortIndentTest` to guard against regressions and verify that property initializers remain indented when wrapping occurs without additional indentation fixes.
- Documented the fix in the Unreleased “Fixed” section of the changelog.

Tests:
- `./gradlew ktlint-ruleset-standard:test --tests "com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.PropertyWrappingRuleBestEffortIndentTest"`

 26→import mu.KotlinLogging
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→
 29→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}

173→public val PROPERTY_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyWrappingRule().ruleId

 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }

149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}

263→/**
264→ * Updates or inserts a new whitespace element with [text] after the given node. If the node itself is a whitespace
265→ * then its contents is replaced with [text]. If the node is a (nested) composite element, the whitespace element is
266→ * added after the last child leaf.
267→ */
268→public fun ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(text: String) {
269→    if (this is LeafElement) {
270→        if (this.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
271→            return replaceWhitespaceWith(text)
272→        }
273→        val next = treeNext ?: nextLeaf()
274→        if (next != null && next.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
275→            next.replaceWhitespaceWith(text)
276→        } else {
277→            PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(text).also { psiWhiteSpace ->
278→                (psi as LeafElement).rawInsertAfterMe(psiWhiteSpace)
279→            }
280→        }
281→    } else {
282→        lastChildLeafOrSelf().upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(text)
283→    }
284→}

286→public val ASTNode.column: Int
287→    get() {
288→        var leaf = this.prevLeaf()
289→        var offsetToTheLeft = 0
290→        while (leaf != null) {
291→            if ((leaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE || leaf.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART) && leaf.textContains('\n')) {
292→                offsetToTheLeft += leaf.textLength - 1 - leaf.text.lastIndexOf('\n')
293→                break
294→            }
295→            offsetToTheLeft += leaf.textLength
296→            leaf = leaf.prevLeaf()
297→        }
298→        return offsetToTheLeft + 1
299→    }

329→/**
330→ * Verifies that a leaf containing a newline exist in the closed range [from] - [to]. Also, returns true in case any of
331→ * the boundary nodes [from] or [to] contains a newline.
332→ */
333→public fun hasNewLineInClosedRange(
334→    from: ASTNode,
335→    to: ASTNode,
336→): Boolean =
337→    from.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() ||
338→        leavesInOpenRange(from, to).any { it.textContains('\n') } ||
339→        to.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→
341→/**
342→ * Verifies that no leaf contains a newline in the closed range [from] - [to]. Also, the boundary nodes [from] and [to]
343→ * should not contain a newline.
344→ */
345→public fun noNewLineInClosedRange(
346→    from: ASTNode,
347→    to: ASTNode,
348→): Boolean =
349→    !from.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
350→        noNewLineInOpenRange(from, to) &&
351→        !to.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
352→
353→/**
354→ * Verifies that no leaf contains a newline in the open range [from] - [to]. This means that the boundary nodes are excluded from the range
355→ * in case they would happen to be a leaf node. In case [from] is a [CompositeElement] than the first leaf node in the sequence is the first
356→ * leaf node in this [CompositeElement]. In case [to] is a [CompositeElement] than the last node in the sequence is the last leaf node prior
357→ * to this [CompositeElement].
358→ */
359→public fun noNewLineInOpenRange(
360→    from: ASTNode,
361→    to: ASTNode,
362→): Boolean = leavesInOpenRange(from, to).none { it.textContains('\n') }
363→
364→/**
365→ * Creates a sequence of leaf nodes in the open range [from] - [to]. This means that the boundary nodes are excluded
366→ * from the range in case they would happen to be a leaf node. In case [from] is a [CompositeElement] than the first
367→ * leaf node in the sequence is the first leaf node in this [CompositeElement]. In case [to] is a [CompositeElement]
368→ * than the last node in the sequence is the last leaf node prior to this [CompositeElement].
369→ */
370→public fun leavesInOpenRange(
371→    from: ASTNode,
372→    to: ASTNode,
373→): Sequence<ASTNode> =
374→    from
375→        .leaves()
376→        .takeWhile { it != to && it != to.firstChildNode }

 32→/**
 33→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a value parameter.
 34→ *
 35→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 36→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 37→ * indentation rule is not run.
 38→ *
 39→ * This rule has many similarities with the [PropertyWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 40→ */
 41→public class ParameterWrappingRule :
 42→    StandardRule(
 43→        id = "parameter-wrapping",
 44→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 45→            setOf(
 46→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 47→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 48→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 49→            ),
 50→    ) {
 51→    private var line = 1
 52→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 53→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 54→
 55→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 56→        line = 1
 57→        indentConfig =
 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
 71→            rearrangeValueParameter(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun rearrangeValueParameter(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 78→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 79→    ) {
 80→        require(node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 81→
 82→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 83→
 84→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 85→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 86→        val fromNode =
 87→            node
 88→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 89→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 90→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 91→                ?: node
 92→
 93→        node
 94→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 95→            ?.let { colon ->
 96→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 97→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 98→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 99→                    return
100→                }
101→            }
102→
103→        node
104→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
105→            ?.let { typeReference ->
106→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
107→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
108→                    return
109→                }
110→            }
111→
112→        node
113→            .findChildByType(EQ)
114→            ?.let { equal ->
115→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
116→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
117→                    return
118→                }
119→            }
120→
121→        node
122→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
123→            ?.let { callExpression ->
124→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
125→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
126→                    return
127→                }
128→            }
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
132→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
133→            .nextCodeLeaf()
134→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
135→            ?: this
136→
137→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
138→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
139→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
140→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
141→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
142→    }
143→
144→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
145→        node: ASTNode,
146→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
147→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
148→    ) {
149→        emit(
150→            node.startOffset - 1,
151→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
152→            true,
153→        )
154→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
155→        if (autoCorrect) {
156→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
157→        }
158→    }
159→
160→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
161→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
162→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
163→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
164→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
165→    ) {
166→        emit(
167→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
168→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
169→            true,
170→        )
171→        LOGGER.trace {
172→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
173→        }
174→        if (autoCorrect) {
175→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
176→        }
177→    }
178→}

0→# Changelog
 1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 3→
 4→## Unreleased
 5→
 6→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 7→
 8→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 9→
10→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
11→
12→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
13→
14→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
15→
16→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
17→
18→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
19→
20→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
21→
22→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
23→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
24→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
25→
26→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
27→
28→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
29→
30→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
31→
32→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
33→
34→### Added
35→
36→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
37→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
38→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
39→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
40→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
41→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
42→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
43→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
44→
45→### Removed
46→
47→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
48→
49→### Fixed
50→
51→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
52→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
53→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
54→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
55→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
56→
57→### Changed
58→
59→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
60→* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
61→* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
62→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
63→* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
64→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
65→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
66→

68→

73→### Fixed
74→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
75→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
76→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
77→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
78→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
79→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
